title: Acronia excavata
taxonomy: Acronia excavata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This genus is a part of the Rubiaceae family, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The plant is native to Borneo, specifically in the tropical regions of the island. Acronia excavata is considered to be an endemic species found primarily in Malaysia and Brunei. Its classification as a species within the Acronia genus is a part of the larger classification system of flowering plants.
